title: Color Palette Registry
description: Single source of truth for color palettes used across AIG-RLIC+ portal charts. Per VIZ-V11 (owner: Vera, per META-CF), every chart's `_meta.json` sidecar must carry a `palette_id` field referencing an entry here. Producer-side lint blocks chart saves when raw matplotlib/plotly default colors appear in trace/shape/annotation color fields instead of a registered palette color. Palette changes between versions are methodological divergence per META-XVC — a bump to palette_id requires a regression_note entry and a sop-changelog entry.

x-version.description: Semver of the registry instance (separate from this schema's x-version). Bumped per META-CF whenever a palette entry is added, renamed, or any color value changes.

x-owner.description: Single agent accountable for the registry instance. Must be 'vera'.

x-generated.description: ISO-8601 date the instance was last regenerated.

notes.description: Freeform top-level notes about the instance (e.g., what was added in this version, migration notes across palette_id values).

palettes.description: Map of palette_id -> palette definition. palette_id is snake_case, versioned (e.g., 'okabe_ito_2026' so a future 'okabe_ito_2027' bump is traceable).

palette_definition.description: One named, versioned palette. The role keys below are canonical — every chart color field must map to one of these roles.

palette_id.description: Echo of the map key (e.g., 'okabe_ito_2026'). Versioned so future palette changes are traceable and so charts can record the exact palette_id used at build time.

description.description: One-line human-readable summary of the palette (source, accessibility profile, intended audience).

source_citation.description: Origin / attribution (e.g., 'Okabe and Ito 2008, Color Universal Design').

colorblind_safe.description: True if the palette is designed to be distinguishable under common colorblind conditions (deuteranopia, protanopia, tritanopia).

primary_data_trace.description: Main data series color (hero indicator trace, zoom chart data line, the headline series).

secondary_data_trace.description: Overlay / second series (e.g., target series on a dual-panel chart, benchmark on an equity-curve chart).

tertiary_data_trace.description: Optional third series color for charts with >2 traces (e.g., strategy vs B&H vs baseline).

nber_shading.description: NBER recession shading fill — rgba with alpha in 0.20-0.28 band per VIZ-V2. Default 'rgba(150,120,120,0.22)'.

event_marker_line.description: Dashed vertical annotation line color for historical-episode event markers (VIZ-V1, VIZ-V12).

event_marker_label_bg.description: Background fill for event-marker annotation labels (typically near-white with alpha for readability over data traces).

buy_indicator.description: Color for BUY / risk-on trigger cards and signal-crossing indicators (APP-SE3).

sell_indicator.description: Color for SELL / REDUCE / risk-off trigger cards and signal-crossing indicators (APP-SE3).

hold_indicator.description: Color for HOLD / neutral trigger cards and signal-crossing indicators (APP-SE3).

equity_curve.description: Default color for the winning strategy equity curve (Strategy page).

drawdown_fill.description: Shaded area fill for drawdown-from-peak charts (VIZ-A3 equity_drawdown).

quartile_gradient.description: Optional ordered 4-element gradient for quartile-return bar charts (Q1 → Q4). Tightest/best regime first, widest/worst last.

categorical_extended.description: Optional ordered list for charts that need >3 categorical colors (cross-pair dashboards, multi-target comparisons).

color_value.description: Color in hex `#RRGGBB` / `#RRGGBBAA` form or rgba(r,g,b,a) form. No named CSS colors, no matplotlib defaults like 'C0'.
